death_salience: The aspect of death that arouses me is that of no longer having to deal with the pains of life from stress over bills, to the struggles with mental health. Of course you'll be dead, but it is an arousing thought thinking of losing all of the pain from the struggles of life.  As for how we die, I believe once we're dead, we're dead.  Just like being put to sleep for surgery, during that moment, you no longer exist and I believe that's the same for death.  Once the lights go out, that's it for you, you body still remains, but you as a person internally no longer exist.  I do not have a strong faith in an afterlife, so I see death as the ending of our story, and once we pass away, it's simply darkness.

I think that as I die, I physically would not want to be alone. Once I am physically dead I think my soul will enter a new state of life and living.

I will probably know that I am dying and once I have died. At least for a short period my brain will know I have died and am dead. Then, my Spirit will leave my body and begin it's journey to The Spirit World. For me, that is with God and Loved Ones and all from a Christian Perspective.
